name: Reading assessment bank
note: Each item embeds a short passage. AI-authored draft; needs human review.

questions.question:
Passage: "Tom works in a bookshop. He starts at 9 a.m. and finishes at 5 p.m. On Sundays the shop is closed."

What time does Tom finish work?
questions.explanation: The passage says he finishes at 5 p.m.

questions.question:
Passage: "The museum offers free entry on the first Monday of every month. Students always pay half price."

When is entry free?
choices: The first Monday of the month
questions.explanation: Free entry is on the first Monday of each month.

questions.question:
Passage: "The recipe needs two eggs, a cup of flour, and some milk. Bake for twenty minutes."

How long should it bake?

Passage: "Many cities are adding bike lanes. Cycling reduces traffic, cuts pollution, and improves health. More people are choosing bikes over cars."

What is the main idea?
choices: Cycling has several benefits for cities
questions.explanation: The passage lists the benefits of cycling.

questions.question:
Passage: "Sleep is essential. It helps memory, mood, and the immune system. Adults who sleep too little often feel tired and forgetful."

The passage is mainly about ___.
questions.explanation: The whole passage explains why sleep matters.

questions.question:
Passage: "Remote work has grown rapidly. While it offers flexibility and saves commuting time, some workers report feeling isolated and struggle to separate work from home life."

The main point is that remote work ___.
choices: is always better than office work
questions.explanation: It presents benefits and downsides — both sides.

questions.question:
Passage: "The hikers were exhausted after climbing for hours."

'Exhausted' most nearly means ___.
questions.explanation: Climbing for hours makes you very tired.

questions.question:
Passage: "The new policy was met with widespread criticism from employees."

'Criticism' most nearly means ___.

Passage: "Scientists observed a gradual decline in the bird population."

'Gradual' most nearly means ___.

Passage: "Maria checked the sky, grabbed her umbrella, and left quickly."

What can we infer?
choices: Maria was going to the beach
questions.explanation: Taking an umbrella after checking the sky implies she expected rain.

questions.question:
Passage: "Despite studying all night, Daniel could barely keep his eyes open during the exam and left several questions blank."

What can we infer?
choices: Daniel was too tired to finish well
questions.explanation: Studying all night left him too tired to perform.

questions.question:
Passage: "The restaurant was packed every night, yet within a year its doors had closed for good."

What does this suggest?
choices: Being busy did not guarantee survival
questions.explanation: 'Yet ... closed' signals that popularity wasn't enough.
